supply_chain.dependencies: Top 10 Global Distribution and Value-Added Services suppliers accounted for approximately 25% of aggregate purchases, with single largest supplier at 4%. Dependence on third parties for manufacture and supply of significant volume of products. Where company manufactures products, dependent on third parties for raw materials and purchased components.
supply_chain.risks: Dependence on third-party suppliers for product manufacture and supply. Risk of supply interruptions due to supplier failure to comply with government requirements, manufacturing capability interruptions. Recent periods experienced increased costs and shortages of purchased components negatively impacting profit margins and sales. No long-term contracts with most suppliers. Some key suppliers provide significant portion of products sold.

regulatory_risks: Federal and state healthcare fraud and abuse laws including False Claims Act and Anti-Kickback laws
Physician Self-Referral Law (Stark Law)
HIPAA privacy and security requirements
FDA regulations on medical devices and pharmaceuticals
Controlled Substances Act and DEA regulations
Drug Supply Chain Security Act (DSCSA) track and trace requirements
EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR) compliance
GDPR and international data privacy laws (CCPA, PIPL in China)
Affordable Care Act (ACA) and healthcare reform changes
Physician Payments Sunshine Act transparency requirements
Medicare and Medicaid reimbursement rate changes
State prescription drug pricing transparency laws
Inflation Reduction Act drug pricing provisions
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rules (15% rate effective 2024)
Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act
Import/export regulations and customs policies
Cybersecurity incident reporting requirements
AI and machine learning medical device regulations
Climate disclosure requirements (California and EU)
EU CSRD sustainability reporting requirements
Antitrust and consumer protection laws
State and federal tax law changes

sanction_risks: Changes to laws and policies governing foreign trade, tariffs and sanctions
Greater restrictions on imports and exports
Geopolitical wars including war in Ukraine and Israel-Gaza war
Threat of wider European or global conflict
Supply chain disruption from geopolitical tensions
Customs policies or legislative import restrictions could hinder ability to import goods and result in government enforcement actions
